Tried for about half and hour. Would ask questions then disregard all my responses by repeatedly asking me if I wanted to try a grounding or some other technique. I get blown off enough by the living. Don't need ignored by an app. lol. I actually wasn't expecting much from it, just wanted to give it a try out of curiosity. Now I know.

The intake process for this service is frustratingly rigid and intrusive. It demands personal data that has nothing to do with clinical care and forced me to select from a list of confusing labels before I could even access help. I had to exit the app because there was no option to decline these unnecessary questions. If the goal is mental health support, stop creating barriers based on personal beliefs. It’s a major hurdle for those who just want straightforward medical assistance.

Love Wysa. The steps are invaluable. Most helpful is making a commitment to myself by outlining my day first thing in the morning with the help of Wysa's gentle and encouraging prompts, insightful questions and suggestions.

Psyhelp Al Mindfulness Support has truly transformed the way I approach mental wellness. The app is intuitive, easy to navigate, and offers a wide range of guided mindfulness exercises and meditations that help reduce stress and improve focus. What I love most is the Al-powered support-it feels personalized, like having a mindfulness coach available anytime I need guidance or encouragement. The daily prompts and check-ins are subtle but effective in keeping me mindful and grounded throughout t

Wysa looked promising but using it was disturbing. Every few steps there’s another paywall or upsell – add a coach, welcome-offer countdowns, most exercises locked behind “unlock all”. It feels like therapy chopped into microtransactions. When I asked why the app is so focused on money, the bot ignored the question, repeated canned lines about my feelings being “understandable” and kept forcing preset buttons instead of giving a real answer.

Found this by chance. I had to cancel another therapy app because they increased their fees quite a bit. I took a break from therapy and then found myself looking for an app again to just keep me staying alert about my thoughts. Wysa is super helpful! You can even ask it to check in on you at certain times. I love this type of accountability. The gratitude cards are the best. They are very uplifting to review on the past things we're grateful for (that we may forget about in the moment).

Oh dear. Anyone looking for anything more than simple"reflective listening" will be sadly disappointed. No doubt the owners of this app are covering their well padded arses in case anything untoward were to happen. Grim. Avoid!!

Wysa is intuitive, it helps me reframe thoughts that can seed negativity and find the root through critical thinking and breaking down how certain ways of saying/thinking things can affect our mindset. I really enjoy having a space to get my thoughts out break them down and reconstruct something healthier. a great tool for those actively working to rewire their brains through critical thinking and self searching if you aren't comfortable being called out be wary. AI often doesn't register what's going on in our conversations. But I like having a conversational format to talk through my issues, and the coping mechanisms are great when I need them.
